content: docker compose exec -T (--no-TTY) disables pseudo-terminal allocation. Without -T, Docker allocates a PTY which adds carriage returns and corrupts binary data. For pg_dump -Fc (binary compressed format), stdout MUST be captured without TTY corruption. The correct pattern: PGPASSWORD=$PW docker compose exec -T database pg_dump -U $USER -Fc $DB > dump.file (stdout redirected on the host side). There is a known issue (github.com/docker/compose issue #8909) where docker compose exec -T can corrupt binary INPUT when piping (used for pg_restore). For pg_dump OUTPUT captured to a file, redirect on the host shell (>) works correctly. For pg_restore INPUT from file, use shell redirect (<) not pipe. docker exec -i (using container ID) is an alternative that avoids compose layer.
